programmes.show_description: Images: Hawkeye and BJ try to discourage Radar from getting a tattoo. Hot Lips realises that there is a camaraderie of nurses at the 4077th and she is not included.
The M*A*S*H Olympics: Col Potter enforces an exercise course. When Klinger hears that being overweight can bring a discharge he decides to put on some extra pounds.
The Grim Reaper: Colonel Bloodworth antagonises Hawkeye, he retaliates by shoving him. Bloodworth presses for a court martial until he watches Hawkeye save a soldier's life.
Getting on?!: After Henry's recent visit to the doctor's, Matthew decides to do more for older people, and invites a local pensioner to come and stay with them.
Rumpole and the Sporting Life: Fiona persuades Rumpole to defend her sister Jennifer, who is accused of murdering her husband.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Monster - Part 1: In this two-part episode, a disfigured artist finds love with a blind girl. Scotty, a quadriplegic lawyer, won't admit to his marital problems.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Monster - Part 2: Julian the 'town monster' is accused of attacking the young, blind Rachel. Jonathan, and Mark's friend Scotty, are on hand to provide legal assistance.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

A Child Of God: Jonathan and Mark try to reunite Marsha with her father but he refuses to see her for having a child out of wedlock. But Marsha doesn't have much time left on Earth.
A Match Made In Heaven: Jonathan's friend Scotty, a quadriplegic, wonders if his relationship with a woman he likes can succeed.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: I'll Ride the Wind: Mr Edwards' son must make a tough decision: realise his dream of being a writer or marry his one true love, Mary Ingalls.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Quarantine: A deadly mountain fever hits the neighbouring town of Elmsville, and Mr Edwards unknowingly carries it back to Walnut Grove.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Little Women: Mrs Oleson rewrites a play so Nellie can have the starring role, but shy Ginny Clark steals the show.
Spring Fever: Mamie Baldwin thinks her declining rosebush is a sign of her own impending death. Meanwhile, Jim-Bob and Ben fight over each other's girlfriends.
The Festival: Jason encounters racism when he and Josh audition for the Spring Festival, and Elizabeth faces a battle to win a boy's affection.
With a Song in My Heart: Dillon finds his decision to see Niamh in Dublin has a much bigger impact than he expected. Aidan's plans for the choir aren't going well.
Love Is Where It Falls: Nancy and Wilf decide to swap jobs, with predictable results, whilst Nancy goes all out to woo Nick. Charlie tries to impress Hettie, but lands in the hospital.
An outbreak of cholera at the Thrift forces Eleanor to notify the medical officer, who surprises her by deciding to treat the victims there rather than cart them off to a fever hospital.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Last Resort: Rumpole defends the owner of a caravan rental business who is accused of fraud. Meanwhile, Rumpole asks Hilda for help to get an elusive solicitor to pay a debt.
Comrades in Arms - Part 1: Lost behind enemy lines, Hawkeye and Margaret form a personal truce and seek shelter in a roadside hut.
Comrades in Arms - Part 2: Potter and BJ are frantic about their missing comrades leading BJ to disobey orders to find them. Hawkeye and Margaret have become more hostile towards each other.
The Merchant of Korea: BJ and Hawkeye challenge Major Winchester to a poker game in the hope that he will stop using the money they owe as leverage for random favours.
Comedy classic with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy. Our bickering duo head to London to celebrate their anniversary, but there's more chaos than canoodling when George is mistaken for a hitman. (1980)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Oh Happy Day: Vicky reckons it's time she and Robin tie the knot. A quiet wedding for two is planned, but her father disagrees. It's his big day too, and he intends to make the most of it!
As Long as He Needs Me: When Vicky leaves Robin to cope with the bistro and moves back in with her ill father to look after him, he fears it's a plan to force them apart.
Comrades in Arms - Part 1: Lost behind enemy lines, Hawkeye and Margaret form a personal truce and seek shelter in a roadside hut.

The Merchant of Korea: BJ and Hawkeye challenge Major Winchester to a poker game in the hope that he will stop using the money they owe as leverage for random favours.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
Rumpole and the Last Resort: Rumpole defends the owner of a caravan rental business who is accused of fraud. Meanwhile, Rumpole asks Hilda for help to get an elusive solicitor to pay a debt.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Good Doctor: While working with a professional football team, Jonathan and Mark learn that one of the players is hooked on drugs supplied by the team doctor.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Alone: A homeless boy wishes that someone would love him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

The Banker And Bum: Melvyn, a cold-hearted tycoon swaps places with Willie, a kind-hearted homeless man to see what it would be like if they each lived different lives.
The Brightest Star: Jonathan and Mark chasten Laurie, a young actress, whose fame is turning her into a monster, fracturing her family and robbing her of her childhood.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Injun Kid: Joseph Stokes is rejected by his grandpa because his daughter married a Native American. However, when Joseph faces some school bullies, his grandpa's opinion changes.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: To Live with Fear: Part 1: Mary is seriously injured and in need of an expensive operation when she is accidentally kicked by a startled horse. Part 1 of 2.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: To Live with Fear: Part 2: Charles accepts a dangerous job in order to pay for Mary's ongoing medical treatment. Part 2 of 2.
The Anniversary: John and Olivia celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary, and Mary Ellen and Curt both fight temptation during their enforced separation.
The Family Tree: With Jason's help, Verdie searches for her family history. Meanwhile, Elizabeth exaggerates the truth when writing to her soldier pen pal.
Love's Labours: When Frankie and Aidan join forces for a day out, Aidan's reputation as a priest is compromised. Liam and Donal are the first to hear a big announcement from Niamh and Sean.
The Element of Surprise: Jean invites Ruby to the Antiques Fair, where Ruby is shot in mysterious circumstances. As Deborah and Nick grow close, Henry offers Nick a job.
Robert's old military comrade Guy Le Saux is back in London, accompanied by his new young Canadian wife, Kathleen, to search out six desperate children for a fresh start in North America.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Old, Old, Story: Rumpole stays with the Erskine-Brown's after an argument with Hilda. Meanwhile, Rumpole defends a man accused of the attempted murder of his business partner.
The Smell of Music: Hawkeye and BJ refuse to shower until Major Winchester stops playing his French horn and Colonel Potter deals with a disfigured patient who wants to kill himself.
Patent 4077: Klinger accidentally discards Margaret's wedding ring and is sent to the dump to find it. Meanwhile, Hawkeye is fed up with having to work with inadequate surgical equipment.
Tea and Empathy: Penicillin has been stolen at the 4077th just as wounded soldiers arrive. Father Mulcahy knows where the penicillin is being stored due to a soldier's confession.
Old-school comedy chaos with Terry-Thomas, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole. A trip to Italy is up for grabs, and nothing is going to stop the terrors of St Trinian's raising hell in Europe... (1957)

The Seven Pound Fiddle: When money goes missing from the till, Nicholls blames Albert and sacks him. But Albert is not guilty, and it's hard to find him to apologise.
Ups and Downs: When they find a cellar under the bistro there are naturally disagreements between Robin and Nicholls as to what they should do with the space. More seating or a wine cellar?
